subject: Public Health
title: Gavi: Vaccinating the World's Poorest
description: A quick win: understand Gavi: Vaccinating the World's Poorest.

hook.text: A child born in a rich country gets 13 vaccines. A child born in a poor country might get none.

buildup.text: Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance, was founded in 2000 to close this gap—pooling funds to buy vaccines for the poorest.

discovery.text: By negotiating bulk purchases, Gavi reduced vaccine prices by up to 95% for developing countries.

twist.text: Gavi has immunized over 1 billion children—preventing more than 17 million future deaths since 2000.

climax.text: Every dollar invested in Gavi-supported vaccination returns $54 in health and economic benefits.

punchline.text: Where you're born shouldn't determine whether you're vaccinated.

quiz.question: How much has Gavi reduced vaccine prices for developing countries?
